dimensions.name: Audience Journey Completeness
dimensions.pass_criteria: The documentation covers the primary user's complete journey with the product — from first access through core use to any required go-live or production steps. The intended audience (developer, end user, admin, partner) could follow the docs end-to-end without needing to contact the product team for basic questions.
dimensions.fail_criteria: One or more major stages of the user's journey are missing or too thin to be actionable. A reader cannot determine how to get started, how to complete the core task, or what the path to production/active use looks like.

dimensions.name: Technical Internal Consistency
dimensions.pass_criteria: Any term, field name, endpoint, setting, or concept that appears in multiple documentation files uses identical naming throughout. A reader following the getting-started guide into the reference docs would not encounter unfamiliar or conflicting names.
dimensions.fail_criteria: One or more terms, field names, or concepts are named differently across files. A reader following from one section to another would encounter inconsistencies that require guessing or backtracking.

dimensions.name: Format Fit for Product Type
dimensions.pass_criteria: The documentation format matches the product type. API/integration products have developer portal-style docs (reference, auth, code examples). User-facing applications have task-oriented user guides. Admin/ops tools have setup and operations guides. The file structure is driven by what the audience needs to accomplish, not by a generic template.
dimensions.fail_criteria: The documentation format is mismatched to the product. An API product has no reference documentation. A consumer application has API docs instead of user guides. The structure feels like a template was applied without considering what this specific product and audience actually need.

dimensions.name: Product Specificity
dimensions.pass_criteria: The documentation is written specifically for this product. The product name, core capabilities, user persona, and key workflows described in the Press Release are all reflected in the documentation. A reader could not mistake this for generic or templated documentation.
dimensions.fail_criteria: The documentation is generic — it could apply to any product of its type. The specific capabilities described in the Press Release are absent or underrepresented. Placeholder product names or capabilities have not been replaced.

dimensions.name: Press Release and FAQ Grounding
dimensions.pass_criteria: Every capability documented traces to something established in the Press Release or FAQ. Open items from the Internal FAQ that affect the documentation (e.g. undecided auth method, unresolved compliance requirement) are explicitly marked [TBD — pre-launch] rather than invented or silently omitted.
dimensions.fail_criteria: The documentation describes capabilities or behaviours not established in the PR or FAQ. Or it contradicts the PR. Or Internal FAQ open items that affect the docs are silently resolved with invented details rather than marked [TBD].
